Conditions are negative status effects that players and creatures use. They include damage conditions (bleeding, burning, confusion, poison, and torment), movement-impairing conditions (cripple, chill, and immobilize), debilitation conditions (blind, weakness, vulnerability, and slow), and disabling conditions (fear and taunt). Certain skills can remove these effects from you and your allies.

Confidential. Jade Brotherhood Eyes Only!___## Security Log (cont'd.)—Prepared by Guard HayaoThe outsider, a low-ranking member of the pirate band dubbing themselves "Aetherblades," approached the negotiation with the stated intention to bargain for some sort of technology. Shortly after discussions began, the pirate jumped up and brandished some sort of device in one hand and drew their sword with the other. The device sent a beam of energy toward one of the perimeter guards, dispelling the mesmeric energy around them, revealing them to be a disguised Kestrel spy.While four of our members leapt to contain the spy, detaining him quickly, the Aetherblade bared his fangs with a growl, yelling, "Treason!" and began to attack the nearest of our brothers. The charr are well known for their ferocity, and to his credit, the Aetherblade cut down three of our number before anyone could react. However, numbers won the day, and we were able to end the pirate's life quickly, then quietly disposed of the body.Our scouts report that the Aetherblades believe we still have their man in custody, and Chul-Moo has tentatively agreed to a meeting with their leader, an asura named Ankka. Chul-Moo believes the entire band of brigands is highly unstable, maintaining their ruse until they can devise an adequate contingency plan.As for the Kestrel, the spy has been imprisoned in one of the upper rooms across from the security room. Chul-Moo suspects that the spy knows more about these Aetherblades and their leader, and he wishes to glean everything he can from the treacherous tengu before making a decision on his fate.

To plant your seeds, equip the appropriate seed pouches from your inventory, then interact with a patch of open soil. Contrary to local legend, Viathan Lake never held a native leviathan population—in fact, it is too small to support even one. This was amply demonstrated when the krait, working to establish a deep settlement in Kessex Hills, used an unknown method to magically translocate their "protector" from the Unending Ocean.    The leviathan quickly depleted its natural food sources and began lunging out of the water to pursue livestock and humans from short distances on land. It was eventually slain by fishers, but it had already been weakened by hunger and apparent krait attacks; it was likely desperate enough to attempt to hunt its worshippers.
